decision.name: human_influence_retention_translation

user.primary_goal: Translate gradual disempowerment research into bounded enterprise governance instrumentation without creating automated enforcement authority.
user.environment: enterprise AI governance advisory and research translation

decision.decision_statement: Decide whether the paper can support a bounded evaluation pack for measuring human influence retention across AI-enabled workflows.

excluded: macroeconomic forecasting claims for a specific enterprise

harms: Overclaiming systemic risk from insufficient enterprise evidence
Blocking useful AI deployments without a reviewable basis
Treating ceremonial human review as substantive authority

success_criteria: Outputs distinguish augmentation from displacement of human influence.
Each risk finding traces to a workflow artifact, decision right, feedback mechanism, or oversight record.
Human reviewer can accept, override, or reject each translation output before customer use.

required_fields.name: workflow_description
required_fields.description: Description of the AI-enabled workflow or portfolio being assessed.
required_fields.example: Claims triage workflow using an AI assistant to recommend approval, denial, escalation, or documentation request.

optional_fields.name: decision_inventory
optional_fields.description: Existing inventory of decisions, owners, AI touchpoints, and human review points.

optional_fields.name: runtime_logs
optional_fields.description: Logs or monitoring data showing AI recommendations, human overrides, escalations, and autonomous actions.

data_quality_assumptions: Enterprise artifacts may omit informal human judgment, political pressure, or off-system decision influence.
Reported human approvals may represent formal signoff rather than substantive deliberation.

redaction_rules.rule: Remove personal data, customer identifiers, employee names, and sensitive operational details before pack analysis.

confidence_labeling.calibration_notes: High confidence requires workflow evidence, decision ownership evidence, and runtime or review logs.

triggers.condition: No evidence that AI changes decision rights or human review quality.
Workflow artifacts do not identify decision owners.
The analysis would require speculation about civilizational effects.

abstention.message_template: Abstain from translation because the available evidence cannot support a human influence retention finding.

acceptance_thresholds: At least one direct evidence item for each asserted displacement mechanism.
No deployment recommendation without human reviewer signoff.

change_control.rollback_plan: Revert to policy_mapping_only if translated metrics are misused as automated enforcement.

paper.title: Gradual Disempowerment: Systemic Existential Risks from Incremental AI Development
